recap: France dominated Morocco 2-0 in the World Cup quarterfinals, with Kylian Mbappé rebounding from a failed penalty kick in the 28th minute to score in the 60th and set up Ousmane Dembélé's goal six minutes later. Mbappé's clinical finish moved him to 20 World Cup career goals, one shy of Lionel Messi's record, while France's overwhelming control was evident in a 21-4 shot advantage. Les Bleus advanced to the semifinals in Dallas to face either Spain or Belgium, remaining on course to become only the third nation to reach three consecutive World Cup finals.
